title: Plan-carried simple FITS spec cache
objective: Move repeated simple FITS raw-u16 eligibility header parsing out of the resident CUDA run hot path by carrying simple FITS specs from metadata scan into the processing plan.

code_changes.metadata_scan: Adds GLASS_SIMPLE_FITS_SPEC to FITS header_summary for simple primary FITS images.
code_changes.resident_selection: Uses plan-carried GLASS_SIMPLE_FITS_SPEC before falling back to file-header probing.
code_changes.artifact_contract: Records spec_source_counts, plan_header_spec_count, and file_header_probe_count in resident_fits_auto_selection.raw_u16_gpu.

cuda.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition

clean_room.statement: The gate uses FITS headers from user input data, GLASS timing artifacts, GLASS output hashes, and user-generated WBPP black-box output/timing. It does not inspect or reuse external implementation source.

known_limitations: The hot-path benefit applies after regenerating scan/plan with GLASS_SIMPLE_FITS_SPEC present.
Legacy plans remain compatible but still use the old file-header probe fallback.
This removes repeated resident-run header probes; the larger bottleneck remains raw file read plus H2D/calibration overlap.
next_step: Continue with a material read/upload/calibrate pipeline optimization, likely native batch file-read orchestration or reduced Python Future overhead, while preserving bitwise 200-light outputs.
